idea-plugin.name: vim-leap
idea-plugin.description:
vim-leap — leap.nvim for IdeaVim

Full port of leap.nvim (and flit.nvim) for IdeaVim. The fastest way to move anywhere on screen without counting lines or reaching for the mouse.

Zero-surprise labels

The moment you type the first character, vim-leap groups every candidate match by its second character and assigns final labels — the same labels you will press after typing the second character. Labels never change between the two input phases. By the time you decide which second character to type, the label for your target is already in front of you. No re-reading the screen, no surprises.

Features

  • 2-char leap — type two chars, jump to a labeled match instantly
  • Stable-label preview — labels are fixed after the first character
  • Safe-label autojump — cursor jumps to the nearest match automatically when matches are few; others stay labeled
  • Label groups — Space / Backspace cycles through groups when there are many matches
  • Traversal mode — Enter / Backspace steps through matches after an autojump
  • Flit (f/F/t/T) — single-char labelled motions with ; / , repeat
  • Equivalence classes — Space matches any whitespace by default
  • Till motions — land one character before/after the match
  • Remote operation — jump, operate, return to origin
  • PSI structural selection — label ancestor nodes, select a structural range
  • Multi-split search — searches all visible editor splits at once

Setup (~/.ideavimrc)

" 2-char leap
nmap s  <Action>(leap.forward)
nmap S  <Action>(leap.backward)
xmap s  <Action>(leap.forward)
xmap S  <Action>(leap.backward)

" Till motions
nmap z  <Action>(leap.forward_till)
nmap Z  <Action>(leap.backward_till)
xmap z  <Action>(leap.forward_till)
xmap Z  <Action>(leap.backward_till)

" Flit — labelled f/F/t/T with ; / , repeat
nmap f  <Action>(leap.flit_f)
nmap F  <Action>(leap.flit_f_backward)
xmap f  <Action>(leap.flit_f)
xmap F  <Action>(leap.flit_f_backward)
nmap t  <Action>(leap.flit_t)
nmap T  <Action>(leap.flit_t_backward)
xmap t  <Action>(leap.flit_t)
xmap T  <Action>(leap.flit_t_backward)
nmap ;  <Action>(leap.flit_repeat)
nmap ,  <Action>(leap.flit_repeat_backward)

" PSI structural selection
nmap gs <Action>(leap.treesitter)
xmap gs <Action>(leap.treesitter)

" Remote leap
nmap r  <Action>(leap.remote)

action.description: Type 2 chars → labels → jump. Forward from cursor.
Type 2 chars → labels → jump. Backward from cursor.
Bidirectional leap in the current window. Targets sorted by Euclidean distance.
Leap forward; land one character before the match (like vim t).
Leap backward; land one character after the match (like vim T).
Label PSI ancestor nodes and jump/select a structural range.
Jump to a label, execute a motion there, then return to origin.
Flit-style f: type one char, labels appear for all forward matches.
Flit-style F: type one char, labels appear for all backward matches.
Flit-style t: type one char, land one character before each forward match.
Flit-style T: type one char, land one character after each backward match.
Repeat last flit jump in the same direction (like vim ;).
Repeat last flit jump in the opposite direction (like vim ,).
